data_prose_consistency.details: wins.json count = 71 entries. Verdict tallies in HTML aria-labels render 'N of 71 claims' for all six verdicts (Refuted by Data 9, Self-Contradicted 14, Misleading 22, Std Model Explains 18, Not Demonstrated 4, Unfalsifiable 4). new_in_v51 = 32 — '32 new WINs' prose in HTML (line 901, 3428, 3492). uncounted-failures.entries count = 10 (4 with dome_status_current = silent, 6 acknowledged-bucket). Scorecard 'Acknowledged Failures: 4' is keyed off DOME_CLAIMED_FAILURES (the dome's own claim of 4 acknowledged failures). All 10 uncounted-failures entries have id/dome_ref/dome_label/what_actually_happened populated.

win_detail_consistency.details: All 71 WIN ids in wins.json have non-empty tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict (covered by test.js). 069 dome WIN numbers covered (001-069), plus sub-entries 057b and 058b for the dome's WIN-057 / WIN-058 collisions.

win_number_alignment.details: Fetched john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html. Dome lists 69 distinct WIN numbers; collisions seen at WIN-057 (SAA Simultaneous Acceleration vs Two-Zone Disc Topology), WIN-058 (Bedrock Induction Ratio vs θ=−lonE Unified Angular Coordinate), WIN-062 (Tesla Wave Speed vs Tesla Constant — wording-only, same claim). Our wins.json covers all 69 dome numbers + 057b/058b sub-entries explicitly capturing the two substantive title collisions; WIN-062 wording-only collision covered with a single entry. No missing numbers, no untracked aliases.

build_reproducibility.details: Cloned repo to /tmp/dome-integrity-clone (commit 963ee69 'Workspace sync: 2026-04-26T08:04:48Z'), ran node build.js html. Resulting docs/index.html DIFFERS from committed docs/index.html by 14 lines (7 insertions, 7 deletions). Three substantive changes: (a) page header date 'April 25, 2026' → 'April 26, 2026' (auto-set during build); (b) WIN-038 Schumann Bessel-mode preempt paragraph clarified — old text claimed dome's TM-mode is bounded ≥5.73 Hz 'as a lower bound', new text correctly states 5.73 Hz is a cutoff minimum (not a ceiling) and that the actual exclusion comes from TM01/TM11 not landing at 7.83 Hz; (c) WIN-064 P-wave shadow zone preempt — internal cross-reference 'but a model that is merely not-incompatible…' replaced by 'but that retreat is also closed, as the paragraph below demonstrates' and a new categorical-binary-test rebuttal added. Cause: data/wins.json + data/sections.json were last touched at 2026-04-26T08:04:48 (commit 963ee69) — workspace-sync push, hours after the last docs/index.html commit at 2026-04-25T21:38:14 (commit 794c58b). Decider has been hitting persistent 403 push failures (7 push-failure-*.json events 2026-04-25T17:28 → 2026-04-26T06:42, all rebuilt-HTML pushes blocked by 'Permission to funwithscience-org/dome-model-review.git denied to Devilwench'). Operator rescue pushes are bringing source data forward but not regenerating HTML. PAT/IP-level issue documented in HNOTE-OPERATOR-PAT-DIAGNOSIS-CORRECTION-001.

issues_found.description: Published docs/index.html does NOT match what the build pipeline regenerates from current data/wins.json + data/sections.json. 14-line diff: page header date stale (April 25 vs today April 26), WIN-038 Bessel-mode preempt paragraph correction unpublished, WIN-064 categorical-binary-test rebuttal unpublished.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Run `node build.js publish` from a clean clone to regenerate and commit docs/index.html. Underlying root cause is the persistent decider 403 push failure (Devilwench denial). Operator rescue-push has been syncing source data via FUSE→git but not the regenerated HTML — operator should run a full `node build.js publish` next rescue cycle, OR the universal-pusher-mode workspace-sync should be extended to push docs/index.html when FUSE has a fresher copy.

summary: Site is mostly sound — internal anchors, tab structure, data-prose consistency, WIN detail consistency, dome-site WIN-number alignment, progressive disclosure, theme colors, expansion tracker (next_id=258 > max_id=257), priority-queue coupling, workspace-sync skip log, digest coverage, and broken-review check all pass or warn-only. The test suite still reports 3452 passed / 0 failed. ONE CRITICAL FINDING: build_reproducibility now FAILS — the published docs/index.html (commit 794c58b 2026-04-25 21:38) is 14 lines behind a fresh build from current data/wins.json + data/sections.json (commit 963ee69 2026-04-26 08:04). Three substantive prose corrections are stranded in source data (page-header date, WIN-038 Bessel-mode lower-bound clarification, WIN-064 categorical-binary-test rebuttal). Cause is the persistent decider 403 push failure ('Permission … denied to Devilwench') — 7 push-failure events in the last 16 hours, with operator rescue-pushes bringing source data forward via workspace-sync but not regenerating HTML. Fix: run `node build.js publish` from a clean clone, or extend universal-pusher-mode workspace-sync to surface docs/index.html when FUSE has the fresher copy. Also flagged: 7 workspace-only analyst scratch files (carry-over moderate), one EXP-052 ID gap (carry-over moderate), and three minor cleanup items (stray .build.js, .bak, __test_fuse.tmp). Yesterday's WIN-013.c4.json workspace-only review HAS been picked up by workspace-sync. Yesterday's intermagnet.org redirect-cancelled flag has cleared (both URLs return 200 OK).
